{{Quote|Enough of this charade!|Artus Imperial warden|<ref name="Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]''</ref>}}
The '''Artus Imperial warden''' was stationed at the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] mining facility on [[Artus Prime]], which was based primarily on [[Slavery/Legends|slave labor]]. The [[Warden/Legends|warden]] was in possession of access codes to the main security doors.

==Biography==
During the [[Battle of Artus Prime]], [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] agent [[Kyle Katarn]] captured the warden and forced him to unlock the doors, allowing the liberated prisoners to escape to a landing pad. As soon as the warden had punched in the code, he grabbed an [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifle]] from a compartment in the wall, and called for a group of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to help him overcome Katarn. The warden and all of the stormtroopers were killed in the shootout.

{{Char-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The warden is canonical, appearing in ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]'', but has never been named. He is referred to in the game as the "Warden". He was voiced by Guy Siner.

According to the tabulation of [[Rank insignia plaque/Legends|Imperial Navy rank plaques]] seen in ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', this officer may have held the line rank of [[Rear Admiral/Legends|Rear Admiral]] and the position of either [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]], [[Commodore/Legends|Commodore]] or [[Commander/Legends|Commander]].

Though this character died, his character model is used many times in other missions.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]'' {{1st}}
